codex_override_catalog.description: Machine-readable Codex treatment map for the full skill catalog.

waves.description: Existing bespoke Codex-first overrides for the execution backbone and shared runtime entry points.
Primary execution and session-continuity skills that anchor day-to-day Codex work.
Analysis and authoring skills where Codex phrasing and artifact expectations materially affect usability.
Open source contribution chain skills that need explicit PR-oriented Codex behavior.
Security review workflows where findings format and gate semantics need bespoke Codex guidance.
Skills reviewed explicitly and kept on converter parity until a real Codex-only divergence appears.

skills.name: compile
skills.reason: The canonical knowledge-cycle contract is already operationally specific; a bespoke Codex prompt is not justified yet.

skills.name: llm-wiki
skills.reason: The proposal is a procedural external-knowledge workflow with no Codex-only runtime divergence yet.

skills.name: autodev
skills.reason: Thin operator layer over ao autodev contract validation; Codex handoff should chain into $evolve or $rpi rather than terminal wrapper commands.

skills.name: beads
skills.reason: Issue routing and dependency edits are a core Codex workflow and need explicit runtime-facing operator guidance.

skills.name: bootstrap
skills.reason: New lifecycle skill; converter parity is acceptable.

skills.name: brainstorm
skills.reason: Codex benefits from a tighter WHAT-vs-HOW split and clearer decision-capture expectations during idea exploration.

skills.name: bug-hunt
skills.reason: Bug audits need Codex-specific findings structure, repro discipline, and validation expectations.

skills.name: codex-team
skills.reason: This skill is inherently runtime-native and must speak directly to Codex sub-agent orchestration.

skills.name: complexity
skills.reason: Complexity analysis in Codex should bias toward ranked hotspots and issue-ready refactor follow-ups.

skills.name: converter
skills.reason: The canonical converter workflow is already tool-specific and remains sufficient without extra Codex prompt tailoring.

skills.name: council
skills.reason: Multi-judge review flow and evidence handling differ materially in Codex sessions.

skills.name: crank
skills.reason: Hands-free execution depends on Codex-native agent control, progress reporting, and scope discipline.

skills.name: discovery
skills.reason: Discovery delegates to sub-skills; default generated prompt is enough.

skills.name: dream
skills.reason: The first Dream skill is a thin interactive layer over the shipped ao overnight contracts, so converter parity is sufficient.

skills.name: design
skills.reason: New lifecycle skill; converter parity is acceptable.

skills.name: doc
skills.reason: Documentation work benefits from Codex-specific emphasis on repo-grounded evidence and validation commands.

skills.name: evolve
skills.reason: The always-on improvement loop needs explicit Codex operating semantics around queues, generators, and persistence.

skills.name: flywheel
skills.reason: Current flywheel monitoring behavior is file-driven and remains understandable from the generated Codex prompt.

skills.name: forge
skills.reason: Transcript mining is largely artifact-oriented and does not yet require Codex-only UX differences.

skills.name: goals
skills.reason: Goal maintenance in Codex should stay compact, measurable, and immediately actionable.

required_markers: Prefer compact status summaries with explicit failing goals, directive gaps, and file-backed updates.
Keep goal maintenance output ready to feed `$evolve`, `$status`, and future Codex sessions.
Prefer measurable fitness language over subjective prose.

skills.name: grafana-platform-dashboard
skills.reason: The canonical dashboard workflow is already highly domain-specific, so converter parity is sufficient for now.

skills.name: handoff
skills.reason: Compaction-safe resume handoffs are runtime-sensitive and need explicit Codex pickup guidance.

required_markers: Capture the current objective, completed work, unresolved blockers, and the next command or file to inspect.
Prefer durable paths, issue ids, and validation evidence over conversational summaries.
Finish handoff-driven session closeout by running `ao codex ensure-stop --auto-extract`; the CLI already skips duplicate closeout for the same Codex thread.
Do not leave the next session guessing what to do first.

skills.name: harvest
skills.reason: New lifecycle skill; converter parity is acceptable.

skills.name: knowledge-activation
skills.reason: The canonical outer-loop contract is already Codex-ready for the first productized rollout.

skills.name: heal-skill
skills.reason: Skill repair is already procedural and script-led; the generated Codex prompt is adequate.

skills.name: implement
skills.reason: Single-issue implementation is a primary Codex workflow and benefits from explicit test-first execution rules.

required_markers: In Codex hookless mode, run `ao codex ensure-start` before implementation begins; the CLI records startup once per thread and skips duplicates automatically.
Work one claimed issue to completion with acceptance criteria, touched files, and validation steps kept explicit.
Prefer TDD or the smallest proving test first when behavior changes, then widen validation only as needed.
Do not widen scope beyond the claimed issue without creating a beads follow-up.

skills.name: inject
skills.reason: This deprecated background skill has minimal user-facing surface and does not justify bespoke Codex phrasing.

skills.name: openai-docs
skills.reason: Official-doc retrieval and citation behavior need explicit Codex tooling guidance.

skills.name: plan
skills.reason: Planning output must feed the Codex execution chain with issue-ready scope and handoff artifacts.

required_markers: beads-ready issues, dependencies, and acceptance criteria
future Codex sessions can pick them up without chat-only context
Keep WHAT and HOW separated

skills.name: post-mortem
skills.reason: Harvested follow-up work and closure semantics need Codex-specific framing.

required_markers: Treat `skills/post-mortem/SKILL.md` as the canonical close-out contract and `skills-codex/post-mortem/SKILL.md` as the Codex-facing artifact.
Keep the council/validation summary concise, then write learnings and harvested work to disk.
Prefer concrete follow-up items that can flow directly into `.agents/rpi/next-work.jsonl` for the next Codex loop.
Own Codex closeout during the post-mortem flywheel phase by running `ao codex ensure-stop --auto-extract`; the CLI already skips duplicate closeout for the same Codex thread.
Keep harvested work machine-checkable: available on write, then claim/release/consume through the queue lifecycle.

skills.name: pr-implement
skills.reason: Fork-based contribution work needs explicit Codex guidance around isolation, scope control, and proof of completion.

skills.name: pr-prep
skills.reason: Preparing a PR in Codex needs a tighter artifact checklist and explicit user-review boundary.

skills.name: pr-research
skills.reason: Upstream research works better in Codex with explicit evidence capture and maintainer-pattern extraction.

skills.name: pr-validate
skills.reason: PR validation in Codex should lead with isolation, regression, and scope-creep findings.

skills.name: pre-mortem
skills.reason: Go/no-go judgment depends on Codex-specific gate framing and concise blocker reporting.

skills.name: product
skills.reason: The canonical interview-and-synthesis loop already defines the interaction strongly enough for Codex.

skills.name: provenance
skills.reason: Knowledge-lineage tracing is largely read-only and does not yet need Codex-specific prompt shaping.

skills.name: push
skills.reason: Validate-commit-push flow must align with Codex gate discipline and remote-verification habits.

required_markers: relevant tests first, commit second, push third, remote verification last
branch is synced with origin at the end
Do not run `ao codex stop` after the remote push; finish session closeout before `$push` if the session is ending.
If push fails, stay in recovery mode until it succeeds or a real blocker is identified

skills.name: quickstart
skills.reason: First-run onboarding should reflect Codex runtime expectations rather than generic converted wording.

skills.name: ratchet
skills.reason: Ratchet commands are procedural and already map cleanly into the generated Codex prompt.

skills.name: red-team
skills.reason: New adversarial validation skill; converter parity is acceptable.

skills.name: recover
skills.reason: Context recovery is runtime-sensitive and needs Codex-specific evidence ordering and resume output.

required_markers: Treat `skills/recover/SKILL.md` as the canonical recovery contract and `skills-codex/recover/SKILL.md` as the Codex-facing artifact.
Rebuild state from files, issues, generated artifacts, and git state before trusting chat memory.
Return recovery output in this order: `Resume Target`, `Evidence`, `Gaps or Conflicts`, `Next Step`.
In Codex hookless mode, run `ao codex ensure-start`; the CLI records startup once per thread and skips duplicates automatically.
Make the `Next Step` directly executable by the current Codex session.

skills.name: release
skills.reason: Release preparation needs explicit Codex operator guidance around gates, boundaries, and tagging.

required_markers: Keep the release boundary explicit: everything up to the tag, with validations and changelog evidence called out.
Prefer deterministic command sequences and clear rollback points over narrative release notes during execution.
Do not run `ao codex stop` after the release commit/tag boundary; finish Codex closeout before `$release` if those artifacts must be part of the release boundary.
Do not blur preparation work with post-tag publishing tasks.

skills.name: research
skills.reason: Parallel exploration and artifact capture differ materially in Codex sessions.

required_markers: In Codex hookless mode, run `ao codex ensure-start` before research; the CLI records startup once per thread and skips duplicates automatically.
Prefer `spawn_agent` / `send_input` / `wait` for parallel exploration.
Write findings to `.agents/research/` with file-level references and concrete evidence.
Keep backend fallback logic explicit: codex sub-agents, then background-task-fallback, then inline.

skills.name: retro
skills.reason: Quick learning capture should reflect Codex session habits and artifact-first closure.

skills.name: reverse-engineer-rpi
skills.reason: The canonical reverse-engineering workflow is already prescriptive enough that converter parity is acceptable for now.

skills.name: rpi
skills.reason: The lead orchestration path and disk-backed state contract require explicit Codex-native operating rules.

required_markers: In Codex hookless mode, run `ao codex ensure-start` before phase orchestration; the CLI records startup once per thread and skips duplicates automatically.
When beads are present, resolve bead IDs before routing; when beads are absent, preserve the current goal or execution-packet objective across phases.

Keep a single lifecycle objective spine across discovery, crank, and validation. Never replace it with a child issue ID or one ready slice from `bd ready`, `bd show`, or `.agents/rpi/next-work.jsonl`.
If discovery does not yield an epic id, invoke `$crank .agents/rpi/execution-packet.json` and standalone `$validation` instead of inventing one.
If `$crank` returns `<promise>PARTIAL</promise>`, rerun `$crank` on the same lifecycle objective until the work is done, blocked, or the retry budget is exhausted.
Orchestrate phases directly in the current session; do not hand RPI orchestration to wrapper commands.
For Nightly, evolve, or auto-prompt goals, inspect the last 14 days of Nightly PRs and scheduled Nightly runs before choosing the implementation slice.
Classify recurring evidence as code-driven, runtime-artifact-only, or corpus-state-bound; prefer a code-driven fix unless the user explicitly asked for corpus maintenance.
Route `bd` unavailability, tag push failures, worktree-disposition friction, and security/eval advisory recurrence as prompt/runtime debt rather than treating them as background noise.
claim, release, and consume semantics exactly
claim before work, consume on success, release on failure or interruption

Do not stop after a partial phase result; only stop on `<promise>BLOCKED</promise>`, retry-budget exhaustion, or final completion. Do not count runtime-only artifact flips or corpus-state flywheel movement as successful code improvement without a tracked source change or explicit operator request. Do not invoke Dream/overnight from RPI; use Dream evidence only as input, and keep code-mutating work in the RPI lifecycle.

skills.name: security
skills.reason: Security review needs findings-first Codex output and clear release-gate semantics.

skills.name: security-suite
skills.reason: Composable security tooling benefits from Codex-specific artifact discipline and policy-oriented reporting.

skills.name: shared
skills.reason: This non-invocable shared-reference skill does not need bespoke Codex prompt wording.

skills.name: ship-loop
skills.reason: Vendor-neutral internal-PR cycle; Codex stays in parity with the Claude SKILL.md after slim-frontmatter conversion.

skills.name: session-bootstrap
skills.reason: Universal init prompt is identical across runtimes by design (fungibility contract, soc-vuu6.25); no bespoke Codex variant.

skills.name: standards
skills.reason: Standards are loaded as reference material and do not currently need a Codex-specific prompt layer.

skills.name: domain
skills.reason: Vocabulary corpus loaded as reference material; entries are vendor-neutral markdown and do not currently need a Codex-specific prompt layer.

skills.name: status
skills.reason: Status output should be concise, operational, and resume-friendly in Codex.

required_markers: In Codex hookless mode, run `ao codex ensure-start` before gathering dashboard state; the CLI records startup once per thread and skips duplicates automatically.
Default to a one-screen layout with three blocks in this order: `Current Work`, `Latest Gates`, `Next Action`.
Use exact issue ids, branch/worktree state, and file-backed artifacts instead of conversational summaries.
Keep the output resumable after compaction.

skills.name: swarm
skills.reason: Parallel worker execution depends directly on Codex-native sub-agent patterns and ownership rules.

required_markers: Assign explicit ownership per worker before spawning: issue id, file set, and expected output.
Use file-backed result handoff under `.agents/swarm/` for consolidation and deterministic merge order.
Do not give two workers overlapping write ownership in the same wave unless the merge plan is explicit.

skills.name: trace
skills.reason: Current trace workflows are evidence-driven and remain sufficiently clear under converter parity.

skills.name: skill-auditor
skills.reason: Two-pass auditor delegates to heal-skill (Pass 1) and runs 8 NEW content-discipline checks (Pass 2); parity is sufficient since the auditor's logic lives in scripts/audit.sh, not the prose.

skills.name: skill-builder
skills.reason: Builder dispatcher (scripts/build.sh) drives all 4 modes (from-scratch, from-template, absorb-external, from-pattern); the prose is reference material with no Codex-specific runtime divergence.

skills.name: using-agentops
skills.reason: The meta-skill already serves as explanatory reference material, so converter parity is acceptable.

skills.name: validation
skills.reason: Validation delegates to sub-skills; default generated prompt is enough.

skills.name: vibe
skills.reason: Ship-readiness review output must match Codex findings-first review ergonomics and repo gates.

required_markers: Prefer findings-first output with exact file references, regression risks, and missing-test callouts.
same gates that block `git push`
lead with defects, risks, and verification gaps

skills.name: deps
skills.reason: New lifecycle skill; converter parity is acceptable.

skills.reason: New lifecycle skill; converter parity is acceptable.

skills.name: scope

skills.name: system-tuning
skills.reason: New utility skill for restoring system responsiveness; converter parity is acceptable.

skills.name: hooks-authoring
skills.reason: Hook authoring is documentation-led and portable across runtimes; converter parity is sufficient until a Codex-only hook workflow diverges.

skills.name: curate
skills.reason: Canonical miner role; converter parity is acceptable while the release train decides which legacy miner shims remain.

skills.name: validate
skills.reason: Canonical validator role; converter parity is acceptable until the legacy validation skills are shimmed or retired.

skills.name: operating-loop-workflow
skills.reason: Ship operating-loop Workflow to plugin users via installer skill; Codex twin redirects to the $rpi chain since Codex lacks the Workflow tool

skills.name: automation-shape-routing
skills.reason: Front-door routing skill that names the deciding axis (Workflow vs NTM vs plain skill) and hands off; the prose is decision guidance with no Codex-specific runtime divergence.

skills.name: workflow-builder
skills.reason: Scaffolds a Claude Workflow JS script from the operating-loop.js template; the authoring guidance is tool-specific reference material with no Codex-specific runtime divergence.
